articles.title: Build 2026: Microsoft unveils Project Solara platform for AI agent devices
articles.description: Microsoft unveiled Project Solara, an Android-based platform for an agent-first future, at Build 2026. This new system prioritizes AI agents over traditional apps, enabling devices to understand requests and complete tasks across services. Solara devices will feature 'just-in-time UI,' dynamically adapting interfaces for seamless interaction with specialized AI agents.

articles.title: Oppo is rumored to be working on an A-series smartphone with a 10,000 mAh battery
articles.description: According to a new rumor from China's Digital Chat Station, Oppo is currently working on a mid-range A-series device with a whopping 10,000 mAh battery. The yet-unnamed phone will also allegedly have a large flat LTPS OLED screen with "1.5K" resolution. Unfortunately, the leaker hasn't mentioned the exact chipset it will be using, just that the chip is built on a 4nm process. Given the mid-range nature of the A-series, it's clearly not going to be a high end SoC. People on Weibo are speculating it might be the Snapdragon 7s Gen 4, or a Snapdragon 6 series SoC, or perhaps the MediaTek...

articles.title: Claude outage: Anthropic says working on fix, and other details
articles.description: India and the US witnessed a significant disruption in Anthropic's AI chatbot, Claude, on June 2. Hundreds of users faced access issues, with Claude Chat being the most affected. While Anthropic acknowledged a partial outage across multiple products, including Claude.ai and its API, the exact cause and recovery timeline remain unclear. This follows similar incidents in April.

articles.title: After repeatedly telling that some entire job categories will be 'totally, totally gone' due to AI, Sam Altman now says that companies who have adopted AI the most are ones hiring the most as ...
articles.description: OpenAI's Sam Altman now contends that AI adoption correlates with increased hiring, not job losses. He suggests companies blaming layoffs on AI might be underutilizing the technology, calling it a "convenient way" to explain cuts. Altman believes human interaction and expertise remain central, with successful AI integration augmenting, not replacing, workers.

articles.title: This day, that year: The patent filed on June 2 that helped make radio, television, wi-fi, and mobile communication possible
articles.description: Guglielmo Marconi, a 22-year-old Italian, filed a groundbreaking patent in London on June 2, 1896, for a system of telegraphy using Hertzian waves. This invention, which enabled wireless transmission of signals through the air, laid the foundation for all modern wireless communication technologies, from radio to mobile phones and Wi-Fi.
